adversarial_tests.altered_tokenizer/metadata: covered by tokenizer determinism + gguf parse (corrupt metadata -> parse error/fail-closed)
adversarial_tests.corrupt_golden_fixture: the run command compares against the golden; a corrupt golden -> [8] mismatch -> exit 3 (fail-closed)
adversarial_tests.note: altered-model-byte initially did NOT change output when a single byte in an UNUSED special-token embedding row was flipped (correct localized behavior); the test now corrupts output_norm (on the critical path for every logit) and the output changes as required

do_not_merge: per directive: B is not merged before Candidate C and the final comparison

pr_state.cause: branched from hawking-pre-seed-final 714294ac; main f1369745 added a Phase-1 ledger section -> NUCLEAR_PASTA_LEDGER.md conflict + stale Cargo.lock; both docs/lockfile only, no source conflict

static_proofs.prompt_is: a CLI constant only; runtime never branches on it

tests_added: tests/audit.rs (6): variable_prompt, variable_length, corrupt_model_byte, corrupt_quant_block, logit_shas_deterministic_and_distinct, tokenizer_multi_prompt
verdict: Candidate B is a REAL self-contained runtime: it parses actual GGUF, reads actual tensors, executes every transformer layer, generates from calculated logits using the claimed Q8_0/Q5_0/Q6_K/Q4_K decoders, handles variable prompts and lengths, and reacts correctly to corruption. It does NOT call hawking-core, does NOT shell out, does NOT hardcode golden tokens, and does NOT special-case the parity prompt. Final-token parity is corroborated by 16 distinct per-step logit checksums.
